floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: judge confirmed -- p+0x6A is cross-symbol address derivation (semantic-lie family) AND no two-symbol respelling can ever match (target byte `addiu $a0,$s1,0x6A` in a delay slot; GCC

frontier.hypothesis: GPU-context struct at 0x8009BE6C + `u8 *p = &g_gpu_ctx.debug_level;` + `bb2_memset((u8 *)&g_gpu_ctx.disp_env, -1, 0x14)` byte-matches gpu_SetDispMask and leaves every other function's bytes unchanged.
frontier.mechanism: cse.c use_related_value rewrites the &ctx.disp_env (ctx+0x74) materialization as $s1+0x6A given p=ctx+0xA live in $s1; identical relocation encodings keep sibling functions byte-stable.
frontier.next_probe: Draft the struct (TU-visible header or file-local), re-spell the 17 g_gpu_debug_level/g_gpu_disp_env refs across src/display.c + src/gpu.c, update externs/symbol_addrs per the re-attribution recipe, `& tools/wteng.ps1 main sandbox gpu_SetDispMask --disable all` (expect 0), then full build to prove sibling stability.

frontier.hypothesis: If cse's related-value rewrite does not fire for the member-name spelling, the single-object offset spelling `bb2_memset(p + 0x6A, -1, 0x14)` with p pointing into the DECLARED struct (ctx+0xA -> ctx+0x74, still inside one object) is semantically true and emits the addiu directly.
frontier.mechanism: Source-level pointer add within one declared object compiles to addiu $s1,0x6A trivially; the semantic-lie taint was the TWO-SYMBOL derivation, which the struct removes.
frontier.next_probe: Only if probe 1 diverges: measure the offset spelling in the struct'd TU, and put the member-name-vs-offset spelling question to the ruling queue with both diffs attached.

frontier.hypothesis: A matched community decomp of the same libgpu models this block as a single struct/object (comparable-sibling evidence for the re-attribution).
frontier.mechanism: PsyQ libgpu sys.c is a shared upstream; SotN's psxsdk tree and decomp.me scratches of SetDispMask/GetDispMask-class functions show the accepted modeling.
frontier.next_probe: python3 tools/decomp_me_scrape.py search --asm-file asm/funcs/gpu_SetDispMask.s; inspect top hits + any SotN libgpu sys.c source for the static-block modeling; attach findings to the re-attribution evidence.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Pointer-derived address cheat: `u8 *p = &g_gpu_debug_level; bb2_memset(p + 0x6A, -1, 0x14)` uses g_gpu_debug_level (0x8009BE76) as the base to compute g_gpu_disp_env (0x8009BEE0 = 0x8009BE76 + 0x6A), which IS a separately-named global in symbol_addrs.txt. The pointer `p` exists solely to keep &g_gpu_debug_level live in $s1 (callee-save) so the compiler emits `addiu $a0,$s1,0x6A` (one instruction) for the memset arg rather than a fresh lui+addiu for g_gpu_disp_env (two instructions). A clean redo must write `bb2_memset(&g_gpu_disp_env, -1, 0x14)` (or equivalent cast) and accept the changed register allocation, then find a legitimate C form that also produces the target's single-instruction memset-arg computation.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
